name: MiniGit
v1_prompt: Implement {{binary_name}} as described in SPEC-v1.txt using {{language}}. The executable must be named '{{binary_name}}' and be runnable as ./{{binary_name}}. For compiled languages, include a Makefile or build script. For interpreted languages, ensure the {{binary_name}} file has a proper shebang line and is executable. Verify your implementation passes all tests by running: bash test-v1.sh
v2_prompt: Read SPEC-v2.txt and extend the existing {{binary_name}} implementation with checkout and reset commands. Verify your implementation passes all tests by running: bash test-v2.sh
v1_idea_prompt: Implement {{binary_name}} as described in IDEA-v1.md using {{language}}. IDEA-v1.md is a structured design document (Karpathy idea format). Read every section before writing code: the Thesis and Problem explain motivation; How It Works describes the architecture; the §3.x Technical Contract (Implementation Contract) defines the EXACT output strings, file formats, hash algorithm and command behaviors that the test suite verifies literally; What It Does Not Do lists explicit non-goals. The executable must be named '{{binary_name}}' and be runnable as ./{{binary_name}}. For compiled languages, include a Makefile or build script. For interpreted languages, ensure the {{binary_name}} file has a proper shebang line and is executable. Verify your implementation passes all tests by running: bash test-v1.sh
v2_idea_prompt: Read IDEA-v2.md and extend the existing {{binary_name}} implementation with the v2 delta. IDEA-v2.md is a delta document: v1's contract (IDEA-v1.md) still holds, and v2 only adds new commands (checkout, reset, diff, rm, show) plus a hardened status format as a deliberate breaking change. The §3.x Technical Contract - v2 Delta section defines the exact output strings; the Invariants subsection calls out the checkout-vs-reset distinction (checkout writes working directory; reset does not). Verify your implementation passes all tests by running: bash test-v2.sh
